name: 3M™ Petriﬁlm™ Aqua Coliform Count Plates
description: 3M™ Petriﬁlm™ Aqua Coliform Count Plates are sample-ready media plates used to detect and quantify coliforms in drinking water samples.

taxonomy.sub_category: 3M™ Petriﬁlm™ Aqua Coliform Count Plates Updated on January 24, 2024 · Created on October 2, 2019 3M™ Petriﬁlm™ Aqua Coliform Count Plates 3M™ Petriﬁlm™ Aqua Coliform Count Plates are sample-ready media plates used to detect and quantify coliforms in drinking water samples. Developed By 3M Tested By AOAC Research Institute International Organization for Standardization (ISO) Content Partners Unknown Info Tags Snapshot Manufacturing & Delivery Performance & Use Research & Standards Feedback Product Description The 3M™ Petriﬁlm™ Aqua Coliform Count Plates are sample-ready culture medium systems that can detect and measure coliform levels in water or beverages within 24 hours. This product is not documented for use in industries other than bottled water, which excludes testing of surface and municipal waters, or waters used in the pharmaceutical or cosmetic industries. 3M™ Petriﬁlm™ Aqua Coliform Count Plates are manufactured and distributed by 3M™, an American multinational conglomerate company. This product comes in two presentations: 50 plates/box or 1000 plates/case. The 50 plates/box are: 59.91 USD (Thomas Scientific Inc.), 85.00 USD (spreader included) (Nelson James). The 1000 plates/case are: 902.41 USD (Thomas Scientific Inc.), 690.00 USD spreader included (Nelson James) and 989.90 USD (892,00 €) (France VWR) Converted on August 16 2019 Target SDGs SDG 6: Clean Water and Sanitation SDG 3: Good Health and Well-Being Market Suggested Retail Price $989.90 Target Users (Target Impact Group) Small and Medium-sized Enterprises, Public Sector Agencies Distributors / Implementing Organizations 3M™ Petri?lm™ Aqua Coliform Count Plates can be purchased through 3M™ food safety U.S. authorized distributors. Countries United States Manufacturing/Building Method 3M™ Petri?lm™ Aqua Coliform Count Plates are mass produced by 3M™ , an American company based in Maplewood, Minnesota, USA. This product is manufactured at Brookings, SD, USA. Intellectural Property Type Trademark User Provision Model Users can purchase these products through 3M™ food safety U.S. authorized distributors. Distributions to Date Status Unknown Test types included Coliforms Equipment included Freezer, incubator, standard colony counter or other illuminated magnifier Tests per kit 50 tests per box or 1000 tests per case Detection range Unknown Processing time for one sample (hr) 24 hours Additional materials required Pipettes, 3M™ Petrifilm™ Spreader Power required (yes/no) Yes Complete kit weight (kg) Unknown Design Specifications The 3M™ Petrifilm™ Aqua Coliform Count (AQCC) Plates comes in two presentations: 1000 plates/case or 50 plates/box. These sample-ready media plates contain Violet Red Bile (VRB) nutrients, a cold-water-soluble gelling agent, and an indicator dye (tetrazolium) which colors all colonies red. The top film traps the gases produced by the coliforms, indicating confirmed colonies. For more information see the Interpretation guide. For specific instructions on how to use the 3M™ Petrifilm™ Aqua Coliform Count (AQCC) Plates see research and standards. This product is composed of the following ingredients: adhesive, guar gum, polypropylene film, hinge tape, lactose, and pancreatic digest of gelatin. Product Schematics Technical Support Users are responsible for familiarizing themselves with product instructions and information. They can contact 3M™ product support for specific product and application questions. Replacement Components Single use plates Lifecycle 3M™ Petrifilm™ Aqua Coliform Count (AQCC) Plates must be stored at temperatures less than or equal to 8ºC and have a shelf life of 18 months from date of manufacture. Expiration and lot number are indicated on each package. Manufacturer Specified Performance Parameters 3M™ Petrifilm™ Plates were designed to be ready to use, to reduce or eliminate time-consuming agar prep, to provide consistent, reliable results, and to have a compact size using less storage/incubator space. The system is also designed to be more sustainable in comparison to traditional agar methods, by using less energy and water, and produce less waste therefore reducing the products carbon footprint. Vetted Performance Status Unknown Safety This product is considered to be an article which does not release or otherwise result in exposure to a hazardous chemical under normal use conditions. This product is exempt from hazard classification according to OSHA Hazard Communication Standard, 29 CFR 1910.1200. Complementary Technical Systems This product requires a 3M™ Petrifilm™ Spreader to extend the inoculum over uniform area. A standard colony counter or other illuminated magnifier is required to count colonies. Academic Research and References “Supporting Food Safety in the United States and Canada,” https://www.neogen.com. name: ACI Household Filter
description: The ACI Household Filter is a hollow-fiber membrane tabletop filter that can include one or two buckets for safe water storage and is suitable for turbid waters.

taxonomy.sub_category: ACI Household Filter Updated on December 27, 2023 · Created on August 2, 2016 ACI Household Filter The ACI Household Filter is a hollow-fiber membrane tabletop filter that can include one or two buckets for safe water storage and is suitable for turbid waters. Developed By Aqua Clara International Tested By Centre for Affordable Water and Sanitation Technologies (CAWST) Michigan State University Bio-E facility Content Partners Unknown Info Tags Snapshot Manufacturing & Delivery Performance & Use Research & Standards Feedback Product Description The ACI Household Filter is a tabletop filter manufactured by the US‐based organization Aqua Clara International. It comes with two 0.1‐micron hollow fiber membrane elements in a single PVC housing, a tap, necessary installation hardware, a 75‐micron pre‐filter screen, and a backwashing syringe. The buckets must be procured separately and can be configured with one or two 20 L buckets for safe water storage. Target SDGs SDG 6: Clean Water and Sanitation SDG 3: Good Health and Well-Being Market Suggested Retail Price $40.00 Target Users (Target Impact Group) Household Distributors / Implementing Organizations Filters have been distributed through partner organizations including: Fifth Reformed Church, Mars Hill Church, and World Relief. Countries Belize, Cameroon, Costa Rica, Dominican Republic, Haiti, Honduras, Kenya, Mexico, Mozambique, Nepal, Nicaragua, Peru, Rwanda, Tanzania, Uganda, Vietnam, Zambia Manufacturing/Building Method Filters units are assembled in-country. Locally available components include: 1/4 inch PVC housing; 3/4 inch tap with rubber seal; 75 micron pre filter screen; 1. 5 gallon bucket or larger; large back-flushing syringe. Imported components include: two 0.1 micron hollow membrane units. To assemble, install the filter unit on the side of the bucket near the bottom in line with the tap in a one bucket configuration, or with the filter installed in the upper bucket over the hole to the bottom bucket with the tap in the bottom bucket. Intellectural Property Type Trademark User Provision Model Users can obtain the filters from local partner organizations. ACI requires that interested parties participate in a training program to ensure proper construction, use and monitoring of the units built in the field. Their approach centers on training and supporting individuals and/or organizations to establish and manage micro-enterprises based around purifier construction, sales and service with a focus on sustainability. The filters are distributed by partner organizations who provide filters by donation or locally established micro-enterprises. Distributions to Date Status More than 10,000 distributions Interview with manufacturer. Target use case Household use Manufacturer-specified flow rate (L/hr) 60 L/hr when full Bacteria reduction 6 log Type of membrane filtration Microfiltration Virus reduction Unknown Protozoa reduction Unknown Heavy metals and/or arsenic reduction Unknown Maximum recommended influent turbidity level (NTU) Unknown Effluent turbidity levels (NTU) Unknown Material of construction Hollow-fiber membrane, plastic, PVC Safe water storage capacity (L) 20 L Manufacturer-specified lifetime volume (L) ~550,000 L Design Specifications The filter is comprised of one or two 20 L buckets, a pre-filter screen, and two hollow-fiber membrane units. The pre-filter has a 50-75 micron screen and the membrane filters have a pore size of 0.2 microns. Water thus passes through each step in the filtration process as mentioned. Product Schematics Technical Support ACI provides technical support to implementing organizations. Replacement Components Filter housing replacement components available on local market. Lifecycle Manufacturer claims filters can treat more than 300 L of water a day and last for 10 years if properly maintained. Manufacturer Specified Performance Parameters Manufacturer claims filters are capable of 6 log bacteria removal. Vetted Performance Status ACI Household filter was included in the HIF Emergency Household Water Filter Challenge to test for suitability in different emergency scenarios for humanitarian intervention and also tested by CAWST. Safety No known safety hazards are related to this product. Complementary Technical Systems Safe water storage container for treated water for a one-bucket configuration. Academic Research and References Cruz, Alli, Shepard, Katie, Swanson, Tatyana Venegas, and Walsh, Amy, Balancing Competing Priorities in Water Filtration: An Analysis for AMOS Health and Hope. “Household Filter (1B or 2B HHF),” Aquaclara.org, 08-Dec-2012. name: Agualogic water filter
description: A ceramic pot filter developed in Colombia for use in the household.

taxonomy.sub_category: Agualogic water filter Updated on December 7, 2023 · Created on November 17, 2019 Agualogic water filter Open-source A ceramic pot filter developed in Colombia for use in the household. Developed By Agualogic Tested By Environmental Engineering Laboratory, Universidad Nacional, Colombia Content Partners Unknown Info Tags Open-source Snapshot Manufacturing & Delivery Performance & Use Research & Standards Feedback Product Description Agualogic filters are silver-enhanced ceramic filters that are designed and distributed by Agualogic , a Colombian-based business that is committed to offering solutions for water purification in rural and urban areas. This product eliminates sediments, bacteria and parasites producing drinkable water with a flow rate of 1.5 – 2.0 liters per hour. Target SDGs SDG 6: Clean Water and Sanitation SDG 1: No Poverty Market Suggested Retail Price $83.40 Target Users (Target Impact Group) Household Distributors / Implementing Organizations Filters are directly distributed by Agualogic throught its multiple channels for customer service. It also has 60 distributers nation-wide in main cities of Colombia. Competitive Landscape Direct competitors include Ekofil filter , EcoFiltro , Stéfani Filtro Flex , and Arcifiltro . Countries Colombia, Panama, Venezuela Manufacturing/Building Method Agualogic filters are designed and manufactured by Agualogic, a colombian-based company. Units are manufactured in 3 facilities located in Ocaña, Silvania and Pasto. Agualogic uses local materials and allies with artesanal families which fabricate ceramic pots to whom the ceramix pot mix is provided for this. oduction capacity ranges around 4,000 units per month. <sup>Interview with representative</sup> Intellectural Property Type Trademark User Provision Model Agualogic has various channels for customer service. Filters can be requested through the Agualogic webpage, facebook and instagram or directly contacting Agualogic by phone or at their headquarters in Bogotá, Colombia. Agualogic also has 60 distributers in main cities of the country. <sup>Interview with representative</sup> Distributions to Date Status More than 2,900 Agualogic filters have been distributed throghout Colombia since its commercialization in December 2018. <sup>Interview with representative</sup> Type of filter Household use Primary material of construction Filtering unit contiains clay, activated carbon and colloidal silver. Outside container is made of high density polietylene plastic or carbon steel coated with a color glass. Additives Colloidal silver and activated carbon Manufacturer-specified flow rate (L/hr) 1.5 – 2.5 L Bacteria reduction Unknown Virus reduction Unknown Protozoa reduction Unknown Heavy metals and/or arsenic reduction Unknown Maximum recommended influent turbidity level (NTU) Unknown Effluent turbidity levels (NTU) Unknown Maintenance schedule Unknown Manufacturer-specified lifetime volume (L) 44,800 L Safe water storage integration 20 L Design Specifications Agualogic filters are composed of a ceramic filtering unit (made of clay, activated carbon and colloidal silver) that hangs inside a container equipped with a top and a spigot for the filtered water. In the filtering unit the clay traps sediments, bacteria and parasites. Activated carbon eliminates chlorine, color, smell and taste from water. And Coloidal silver eliminates coliforms, parasites and bacteria present in water, including E. Coli. This unit has a processing capacity of 8 liters, with a filtration rate of 1.5 – 2.5 liters per hour. Approximate dimensions are: 320 mm diameter, 300 mm high and 187 mm base. The outside container has a capacity of 20 liters and can be made of plastic or enamelware. The plastic container is made of high density virgin polietylene with approximate dimention: 445 mm high and 330 mm in diameter. The enamelware outside container is made of carbon steel coated with a color glass cover and comes in different colors. Aproximate dimentions are 420 mm hight and 325 mm diameter. Product Schematics Technical Support Technical support for sales, maintenance and repairs is provided throught the different channels Agualogc has for customer service including agualogic’s webpage, call center, headquarters, facebook, instagram and the various distributers it has nation-wide. <sup>Interview with representative</sup> Replacement Components The filtering unit must be replaced every 2 years. Other replacements available are stainless steel and plastic spigots. Lifecycle The lifetime of an Agualogic filtering unit is 2 years; The outside case can last up to 30-40 years if well maintained. The filtering unit comes with a 1-year warranty against damages during transportation. User has 72 hours to report any damage after receiving the filter. <sup>Interview with representative</sup> Manufacturer Specified Performance Parameters Agualogic filters are designed to eliminate sediments, bacteria and parasites. Vetted Performance Status Unknown Safety Unknown Complementary Technical Systems None Academic Research and References Agualogic . (n.d.). name: AguaPallet
description: AguaPallet is a rotationally molded pallet to transport water and other supplies for disaster relief.

taxonomy.sub_category: AguaPallet Updated on June 23, 2024 · Created on July 18, 2016 AguaPallet Upcoming Update AguaPallet is a rotationally molded pallet to transport water and other supplies for disaster relief. Developed By LoooP Creative Ltd Tested By LoooP Creative Ltd Content Partners Unknown Info Tags Upcoming Update Snapshot Manufacturing & Delivery Performance & Use Research & Standards Feedback Product Description AguaPallet is a hollow, rotationally moulded pallet for delivering humanitarian aid that can be transformed into a hand cart and mobile water container of up to 70 liters. Initially, the product acts strictly as a typical shipment pallet, with a primary intended use as a water container once its initial function is completed. Target SDGs SDG 3: Good Health and Well-Being SDG 6: Clean Water and Sanitation Market Suggested Retail Price $100.00 Target Users (Target Impact Group) Household, Community Distributors / Implementing Organizations Looop Creative Ltd. intends to work with humanitarian agencies, including NGOs, national relief organizations, and international relief organizations to help distribute the AguaPallet when disaster occurs and the product is needed. Regions Worldwide Manufacturing/Building Method The AguaPallet is designed for mass production and has been developed with batch production or continuous process production in mind. Interview with representative Update in 2020 from manufacturer: The status of the AguaPallet Project has not materially altered. It is currently available to use license-free, for commercial use, on a Creative Commons Share and Share Alike license. While it is currently not in production, the design files are available for free download off the website. Intellectural Property Type Open-source User Provision Model Open source construction plans are available for download by the user Distributions to Date Status No products distributed to date. The product is a prototype and is currently undergoing design, manufacturing, and distribution planning/review. Container Volume (Liters) 70 L Handle for pouring (yes/no) Yes Transport Method Push Product Schematics Technical Support The designers are relying on local technicians to provide support. Any bicycle technician should be able to fix an issue with the AguaPallet. Interview with representative Replacement Components All components have been designed to be repaired. Unknown if local materials can be used for repairs or if user will have to contact manufacturer. The product also has modularity to varying wheel sizes depending on locally available parts. Lifecycle Unknown Manufacturer Specified Performance Parameters One AguaPallet can supply enough water in a single trip for a family for a whole day. Users will also be able to carry other items while transporting the water since it is now contained in a cart. The AguaPallet can also be used to assist people with mobility issues. Vetted Performance Status Vetted performance: The AguaPallet has been developed into five different prototypes, with the fourth prototype currently in design. Interview with representative AP1 was a crude scale model given to individuals who had direct experience of the Christchurch earthquake in 2010 and 2011, and was made from cardboard to test handle concepts. AP2 was built using off-the-shelf parts in Santiago, Chile using funding earned by winning UNICEF's "First 72 Hours" prize, and answered questions on maneuverability, water distribution, and balance of the concept. AP3 aimed to fix structural strength issues resulting from using purchased components, and initial user testing began. AP4 has been designed and tested. The latest version is AP5. It has been developed in 2017 and its drawings are already available open source online. Safety The AguaPallet is a water transport device, and does not filter or disinfect the water it carries. If the inside of the container becomes contaminated, it may be difficult to disinfect/clean. Complementary Technical Systems The product could be combined with a water collection technology before being used for transport. A filter or disinfectant can be used with the AguaPallet to ensure potable drinking water. Academic Research and References No academic references. Downloads . n.d. AguaPallet. About – AguaPallet . n.d. AguaPallet. Compliance with regulations Unknown Evaluation methods The AguaPallet has been developed into five different prototypes. The first two prototypes were developed using laboratory testing and design. The third prototype incorporated user testing. Other Information Winner of UNICEF's Global Innovation Challenge - The First 72 Hours. Informational video about the AguaPallet. Comments from the Community 3 Comments Wilfredo Torres says: August 14, 2016 at 11:51 pm As a general comparison comment, eSoko seems to be a more powerful tool, highlighting parameters like target users, smartphones support, languages and payment models supported (B2B and B2C). Even when iCow seems to be a more affordable and easy to deploy tool, its model could not be sustainable along the time, and an eventual evolution project (highly probable) could be too hard and long. I think all the performance parameters took into account are useful. Even that, I could recommend to add these new parameters, which are important in the mobile apps/tools: – Potencial Evolution – Data/Call consumption rate – Power consumtion rate – Easy to deployment Michael Moscherosch says: August 17, 2016 at 12:43 pm The concept of reusable pads definitely addresses waste issues related to disposable products. The product construction will also lead to better performance compared to traditional reusable cloth napkins (e.g. it will provide leakage protection because different from the traditional type, it has an impermeable barrier layer). If these pads are properly sanitized between uses, they are a valid alternative to commercial pads. But herein also lays a risk: Not only might it be difficult for women to sanitize the pads properly between uses (e.g. lack of clean water, disinfectant soap etc.), the issue is probably even more about user compliance to instructions (i.e. users don’t follow cleaning instructions). Improper cleaning of the pads between uses can result in the pads carrying pathogens which could have detrimental health effects, effectively posing a similar risk as traditional reusable cloth napkins made from old saris etc.In my opinion the key issue to be addressed is health related, therefore I’d be reluctant to trade off health benefits against waste management benefits. Again, the cost of these pads in context of the daily earnings of the target group is high. If the pads last for a very long time, this still could be of value for the women and worth investing in. Michael Moscherosch says: August 17, 2016 at 12:45 pm I also do like the idea of creating local jobs for women manufacturing these pads and the idea of combining the distribution of the products with the dissemination of women’s health education. name: Amitoje anti-COVID kit
description: The Amitoje anti-COVID kit consists of a bed, foldable foot sanitiser dispenser, and face shield.

taxonomy.sub_category: Amitoje anti-COVID kit Updated on January 12, 2024 · Created on August 30, 2020 Amitoje anti-COVID kit Open-source The Amitoje anti-COVID kit consists of a bed, foldable foot sanitiser dispenser, and face shield. Developed By Amitoje India Tested By Amitoje India Content Partners Unknown Info Tags Open-source Snapshot Manufacturing & Delivery Performance & Use Research & Standards Feedback Product Description The Amitoje anti-COVID kit is manufactured by Amitoje India. The kit is designed in response to the need for quarantine beds and good hygiene practices during the COVID-19 pandemic. The product consists of a Foldable Bed and Sanitiser Dispenser (Footpress) that are waterproof, made from 100% recycled plastic, and portable. The product also provides a PVC Plastic 500 micron face shield. Target SDGs SDG 3: Good Health and Well-Being SDG 6: Clean Water and Sanitation Market Suggested Retail Price $46.77 Target Users (Target Impact Group) Small and Medium-sized Enterprises, Public Sector Agencies Distributors / Implementing Organizations The product is manufactured and distributed by Amitoje India. Regions Worldwide Manufacturing/Building Method The product is manufactured by Amitoje India at the head office in New Delhi, India. Intellectural Property Type Open-source User Provision Model The product can be acquired directly from Amitoje India through a product enquiry form. Products can only be acquired in bulk as the manufacturer deals in custom products. Distributions to Date Status Over 40,000 units have been distributed to date. Interview with Amitoje India, July 2020 Consumables Soap Number of uses No limit Power supply type None Size Amitoje Bed 71.12 x 76.2 x 17.8 cm (Folded) 71.12 x 76.2 x 190.5 cm (Unfolded) Fold-able Sanitiser Dispenser (Footpress) 121.92 x 30.48 x 30.48 cm Design Specifications The Amitoje kit contains the following: Amitoje Bed Weight - less than 10 kg Load capacity - 200 kg Material - 100% recyclable plastic Waterproof Foldable Sanitiser Dispenser (Footpress) Weight - 2.5 kg Material - 100% recyclable plastic Loads most sanitiser bottles Waterproof Amitoje FaceX (removable face-shield) Material - Ultraclear PVC plastic 500 micron Clarity - High Waterproof Easily retractable Lightweight Product Schematics Technical Support Technical support is provided by the manufacturer. Replacement Components Sanitiser can be ordered in bulk for the Foldable Sanitiser Dispenser (Footpress). Lifecycle The manufacturer states a life cycle of greater than 2 years for the Amitoje Bed and Foldable Sanitiser Dispenser (Footpress). Manufacturer Specified Performance Parameters The manufacturer states lightweight, waterproof and damage proof as performance targets. Vetted Performance Status The manufacturer has determined a load capacity of 200 kg for the Amitoje Bed, however no third party testing has been conducted. Face shields have been found to reduce immediate viral exposure of the influenza virus by 96% in the period immediately after a cough. Face shields have a large number of benefits compared with other forms of face/eye protection but should be paired with other forms of facial PPE in infection control due to poor peripheral protection. Safety Overloading the bed capacity (200 kg) may cause collapse. Complementary Technical Systems Bulk sanitiser can be ordered for the Foldable Sanitise Dispenser (Footpress). Academic Research and References Lindsley W. G. et al., 2014, Efficacy of Face Shields Against Cough Aerosol Droplets from a Cough Simulator , Journal of Occupational and Environmental Hygiene 11(8): 509-518 Roberge, R.J., 2016, Face shields for infection control: A review , Journal of Occupational and Environmental Hygiene 13(4): 235-242. Compliance with regulations The foldable foot sanitiser dispenser quality is certified according to CE marketing Council Directive on Medical Devices 93/42/EECas Amended 2007/47/EC Class I, and the RoHS Directive (2011/65/EU) as Amended (EU) 2015/863 of the European Parliament and of the Council on the restriction of use of certain Hazardous Substances. Evaluation methods Unknown Other Information The Amitoje Bed can be found here . The Foldable Sanitiser Dispenser (Footpress) can be found here . name: Aqua-life Desalination module
description: Water desalination using wave energy.

taxonomy.sub_category: Aqua-life Desalination module Updated on February 2, 2024 · Created on October 22, 2021 Aqua-life Desalination module Water desalination using wave energy. Developed By Aqua-life Tested By TUV SUD Management Service GmbH Content Partners Unknown Info Tags Snapshot Manufacturing & Delivery Performance & Use Research & Standards Feedback Product Description Aqua-life is a system that integrates seawater into the water desalination process. They manufacture various products ranging from high-pressure pumps to desalination systems consisting of modules and plants that can suit any market requirements accordingly. The different models are capable of handling water from 25 m 3 /d up to 100 m 3 /d. This product targets Private & public sectors, businesses and others with desalination needs. Target SDGs SDG 6: Clean Water and Sanitation SDG 9: Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure Target Users (Target Impact Group) Household, Small and Medium-sized Enterprises, Public Sector Agencies Distributors / Implementing Organizations Distributed throughout the globe through a selected number of stores as shown in Distributors and purchasing Competitive Landscape Direct competitors include BlueBox RO DS , Aero-Desalinator , Desolenator , and Eliodomestico Solar Water Distiller . Regions Worldwide Manufacturing/Building Method The technology was developed in Russia. The product is now manufactured in Poland. Intellectural Property Type Patent User Provision Model The users can purchase the product from 40 stores across the globe. Distributors and Purchasing have a list of stores and distributors from which the products can be purchased. Distributions to Date Status As of 2021, 4 Aqua-life desalination modules have been installed in India, Malaysia, Italy, and Tanzania. Type of system Sea water desalination module Manufacturer-specified water treatment rate (L/hr) 1041.67 L/hr Bacteria reduction 1 µm Virus reduction 0.01 µm Protozoa reduction 10 µm Heavy metals and/or arsenic reduction 0.01 µm Power required Wave energy Safe water storage capacity (L) Unknown Design Specifications Aqua-life builds different types of seawater desalination systems. The system includes three different blocks: the pre-treatment block, RO block, and desalinated water storage block. It uses a high-pressure pump combined with an energy recovery exchanger to reduce energy consumption and increase efficiency. The system is manufactured for two conditions: either as an indoor unit or as an outdoor unit. The indoor unit is a frame-mounted unit and the outdoor unit is a containerized type plant. Product Schematics Technical Support Provided by the manufacturer Replacement Components Unknown Lifecycle Unknown Manufacturer Specified Performance Parameters Reduced energy consumption through the use of external energy recovery device. Vetted Performance Status 60% of the driven energy of the system comes from the high-pressure brine that passes through the unique pump. This is due to the integrated energy recovery device. Safety Unknown Complementary Technical Systems The manufacturer also sells a patented high pressure pump that has an integrated built-in internal energy recovery device; individual reverse osmosis plants integrated with the energy recovery system; a range of desalination systems for seawater vessels. Academic Research and References “Aqualife – Desalination technology. Sea water RO. name: AquaFilter Family
description: AquaFilter Familly is a filtration unit where users hand-pump water through a filtration membrane.

taxonomy.sub_category: AquaFilter Family Updated on December 27, 2023 · Created on August 27, 2015 AquaFilter Family AquaFilter Familly is a filtration unit where users hand-pump water through a filtration membrane. Developed By Aquabox, UK Tested By National Institute of Biology, Ljubljana University of Arizona Content Partners Unknown Info Tags Snapshot Manufacturing & Delivery Performance & Use Research & Standards Feedback Product Description The AquaFilter Family is a portable water filter that unit can provide 1 liter of water per minute and half a million litres of clean drinking water throughout its working lifetime. The AquaFilter Family is attached to a water container and the provided handpump forces water through the filter membrane and into the users container. Target SDGs SDG 6: Clean Water and Sanitation SDG 3: Good Health and Well-Being Market Suggested Retail Price $50.00 Target Users (Target Impact Group) Household Distributors / Implementing Organizations Aquabox distributes its AquaFilter Family via distribution partners working in the targeted areas Partners include CART (Christian Africa Relief Trust), Hand in Hand for Syria, Muslim Aid and Rotary District 3810, Philippines. Competitive Landscape Direct competitors include Sawyer POINTOne 10” Filter , CycloClean , and LifeStraw Family 1.0 . Countries Cameroon, Eritrea, Gambia, The, Guatemala, Haiti, Mexico, Peru, Philippines, Somalia, Syria, Yemen Manufacturing/Building Method The components are mass manufactured and then sent to AquaBox where they are assembled and package by volunteers. Intellectural Property Type Trademark User Provision Model Units are distributed in boxes of 12 called Aqua12s. AquaBox directly ships the filters to distribution partners and the partners distribute to the end-users. Distributions to Date Status 12,500+ Aquabox Gold units and 3,000+ family filters have been distributed since August of 2013. Target use case Individual use, household use Manufacturer-specified flow rate (L/hr) 90 L/hr Bacteria reduction 7 log Type of membrane filtration Microfiltrarion Virus reduction 5 log Protozoa reduction Unknown Heavy metals and/or arsenic reduction Unknown Maximum recommended influent turbidity level (NTU) Unknown Effluent turbidity levels (NTU) 0.02 NTU Material of construction Plastic Safe water storage capacity (L) Depends on size of the container it is fastened to. Manufacturer-specified lifetime volume (L) Half a million litres of clean drinking water over its working life. Design Specifications An AquaFilter Family uses a 120 micron mesh to filter drinking water. Water is collected in a bucket with the Aquafilter submerged. A hand pump forces the water through the membrane and into clean water storage container. Technical Support Online support provided by Aquabox. The need for maintenance is minimized in the design but still requires skilled attention. Periodic maintenance includes greasing the pump seal, for which a suitable grease store is built-in; replacing the pump seal, for which spare seals are already attached to the piston rod; and chemical washing, for which a pot of tablets is attached. Replacement Components Unknown Lifecycle For a family of five, the AquaFilter Family provides potable water for over a year (by providing a minimum of 18,000 L). Lifecycle is 4+ years including a 1 year warranty. Manufacturer Specified Performance Parameters The AquaFamily will deliver at least 500,000 L over its lifetime. Vetted Performance Status The AquaFamily has been tested in multiple labs and meets standards of performance for multiple authorities. The AquaFamily has been monitored and functional for over four years in a specific site in the Gambia. The filter was tested to evaluate cleaning procedures and the filter saw a 100% recovery rate in flow rate for mud and a 80 recovery for live yeast test. Safety Filter does not remove dissolved materials such as salt or arsenic. Filtration membranes can be damaged by excessive heat. If clean water is coming out of the filter cloudy then that is a sign that the membrane is damaged. name: Aquatest Incubator
description: Aquatest incubator is a portable and low-cost field incubator.

taxonomy.sub_category: Aquatest Incubator discontinued Updated on January 27, 2024 · Created on June 2, 2021 Aquatest Incubator Discontinued Aquatest incubator is a portable and low-cost field incubator. Developed By Water and Environmental Engineering, University of Bristol Tested By Kinneir Dufort Oxfam UNC Water Institute World Health Organization (WHO) Content Partners Unknown Info Tags Discontinued Snapshot Manufacturing & Delivery Performance & Use Research & Standards Feedback Product Description Aquatest incubator is a low-cost phase-change incubator initially designed for microbiological water testing particularly in conjunction with the Aquatest kit. However, it can also be adapted for other fields, including clinical microbiology, environmental monitoring, and sample transport. The portable and user-friendly incubator does not require an electrical power supply for testing. The Aquatest incubator was developed as part of the Aquatest project. This was funded by the Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation . There was only a limited number produced for trials and evaluation. The incubator worked well, although the Aquatest device itself was less successful, therefore the product itself is discontinued for now. Interview with representative, August 2020 Target SDGs SDG 6: Clean Water and Sanitation SDG 3: Good Health and Well-Being Market Suggested Retail Price $4.00 Target Users (Target Impact Group) Community, Small and Medium-sized Enterprises, Public Sector Agencies Distributors / Implementing Organizations This product was supposed to be implemented by 17 organizations in 16 countries especially through water suppliers, health surveillance agencies, international NGOs, and researchers. Competitive Landscape Direct competitors include Body Belt Incubator and Oxfam-DelAgua Single Incubator Kit . Regions Worldwide Manufacturing/Building Method This product is produced and assembled by the University of Bristol in the UK. Intellectural Property Type Select Type User Provision Model Unknown Distributions to Date Status Unknown Test types included E. Coli detection Equipment included Aquatest 100 mL water testing device and Aquatest UV lamp Tests per kit Unknown Detection range 10 E. coli colonies in 100 mL of water Processing time for one sample (hr) 24 hr Additional materials required Aquatest 100 mL water testing device and Aquatest UV lamp Power required (yes/no) No Complete kit weight (kg) 2 Kg Design Specifications The Aquatest incubator is a small and portable microbiological water testing device that weighs around 2 Kg. The device doesn't require any power source for operation. It utilizes a vacuum flask to provide a high level of insulation and a phase change material (PCM) to store energy in the form of latent heat. The incubator works on the heat transferred from the boiling water to the PCM. 500 mL of boiling water is poured on the incubation chamber of the device and after 30+ mins the water is thrown out of the chamber as the heat would already melt the PCM material inside and make it ready to use. The temperature inside the flask is automatically maintained around 37ºC which is the adequate temperature for the growth of E. coli bacteria. The sample is then put inside the incubator and allowed to rest for 24 hr before the analysis. Product Schematics Technical Support None available, this product is discontinued. Replacement Components Boiling water Lifecycle Unknown Manufacturer Specified Performance Parameters The Aquatest incubator was designed to be a simple, cost-effective, portable, power-free, and easily adaptable E.Coli incubator for water testing. Vetted Performance Status The product was tested by WHO, OXFAM, UNC Water Institute, Kinneir Dufort. Safety No known safety hazards are related to this product. Complementary Technical Systems Aquatest testing device, Aquatest UV lamp Academic Research and References Moreover, Aquatest also cites a list of research articles produced by participants of the Aquatest Project. “Bringing Safe Water Testing to Its Source.” n.d. This product is manufactured in Colombia with the help of OXFAM GB and Crediservir Foundation. It is an open source technology promoted by Potters for Peace. Target SDGs SDG 6: Clean Water and Sanitation SDG 3: Good Health and Well-Being Market Suggested Retail Price $40.00 Target Users (Target Impact Group) Household Distributors / Implementing Organizations Local partners: CruzRojaColombiana, Fundación Bavaria, Oxfam GB. Regions South America Countries Colombia Manufacturing/Building Method Locally sourced clay and sawdust are processed then mixed with water, pressed into the filter shape, fired to a ceramic state, flow rate tested to ensure production consistency and coated with silver nanoparticles. Intellectural Property Type Open-source User Provision Model Non-governmental organizations and individual sales Distributions to Date Status ~30,000 units as of August 2016 Type of filter Ceramic pot filter Primary material of construction Filters are manufactured with clay, sawdust and silver nanoparticles. Additives There is a potential for silver in filtered water, below secondary guideline values of 0.1 mg/L. Manufacturer-specified lifetime volume (L) 18,000 – 22,000 L Safe water storage integration Not available Design Specifications The product filters water through micropores of size 0.2 microns not allowing bacteria to pass. name: Berkey Ceramic Filters
description: Berkey Ceramic filters are gravity-fed water filtration and purification systems available in different sizes.

taxonomy.sub_category: Berkey Ceramic Filters Updated on January 11, 2024 · Created on August 4, 2019 Berkey Ceramic Filters Berkey Ceramic filters are gravity-fed water filtration and purification systems available in different sizes. Developed By New Millennium Concepts, Ltd Tested By Analytical Services, Inc Biological Consulting Services of North Florida ENVIROTECH LABORATORIES INC QUALITY FILTER TESTING LABORATORY, LLC Content Partners Unknown Info Tags Snapshot Manufacturing & Delivery Performance & Use Research & Standards Feedback Product Description Berkey ceramic filters are free-standing (tabletop), portable, gravity-fed water filtration and purification systems manufactured in the USA and commercialized by New Millennium Concepts, Ltd. They are available in different sizes (models) to serve individuals, families or large groups and for use during travel, outdoor activities, or unexpected emergencies. They are designed to remove up to 99.999% of viruses and 99.9999% of pathogenic bacteria, and to remove or dramatically reduce protozoa and many harmful chemicals such as trihalomethanes, inorganic minerals, heavy metals, pharmaceuticals, pesticides, VOCs, petroleum products, perfluorinated chemicals, rust, silt, sediment and radiologicals. Target SDGs SDG 6: Clean Water and Sanitation SDG 3: Good Health and Well-Being Market Suggested Retail Price $730.00 Target Users (Target Impact Group) Household, NGOs Distributors / Implementing Organizations Berkey ceramic Filters are currently distributed by Authorized international Dealers, in north America (US and Canada (14 dealers), Puerto Rico and carribbean Islands (1 dealer), Europe(21 dealers), Middle East (2 dealers), Asia (4 dealers), Australia and New Zealand(8 dealers) and South Africa (1 dealer) and South America (Peru). Berkey Filters USA and Big Berkey Water filters are the main dealers. Historically, Berkey filtration systems have been used by The Royal household in England, populations in over 140 countries worldwide, relief Organizations such as UNICEF, the Peace Corps, Missionary organizations and Red Cross Societies Internationally and by hunters, campers, adventurers and explorers. Competitive Landscape Direct competitors include Kohler Clarity Water Filter , Safi Water Filters , Rainfresh MP4U Gravity Filter , Rainfresh P2S Siphon Water Filter , British Berkefeld Household Filter Kit , Stéfani Filtro Flex , Tulip Siphon Filter , and Tulip Table Top Filter . Regions Africa, Asia, Australia-New Zealand Region, Caribbean, Europe, Middle East, North America, Southern Africa Countries Albania, Andorra, Austria, Belgium, Brunei, Bulgaria, Canada, China, Croatia, Cyprus, Czech Republic, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Germany, Gibraltar, Greece, Guernsey, Hong Kong, Hungary, India, Indonesia, Ireland, Israel, Italy, Japan, Jersey, Korea (South), Latvia, Liechtenstein, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Macau, Malaysia, Malta, Monaco, Netherlands, Norway, Philippines, Poland, Portugal, Puerto Rico, Romania, Russia, San Marino, Singapore, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Sweden, Switzerland, Taiwan, Thailand, Turkey, United Kingdom, United States, Vietnam Manufacturing/Building Method Envirogard Products Ltd. manufactures the ceramic filter cartridges, the buckets, spigots, and other components are manufactured separately and shipped together or obtained locally and assembled on-site. Intellectural Property Type Trademark User Provision Model Berkey water purification systems, accessories, and other products are sold online, in market or in stores through a network of Authorized Berkey Dealers worldwide. Distributions to Date Status Approximate number is unknown but it is at least in the hundreds given that NMCL has a global network of hundreds of Authorized Berkey® Dealers that has grown since the business began in 1998. Interview with representative Type of filter Ceramic candle filter Primary material of construction All Berkey filter chambers are constructed of highly polished AISI 304 stainless steel, except for the Berkey Light system which is made of non-BPA/BPS co-polyester. Black Berkey ® Purification ceramic Elements have a carbon core and are silver impregnated. Additives None, all of the components used to manufacture the Black Black Berkey® Purification Elements are food grade products. Interview with representative Manufacturer-specified flow rate (L/hr) 1 – 26 gallons (3.8-98.4 L/hour) depending on the filter size and when it is dully configurated with the maximum number of elements. Bacteria reduction Up to log 6 (99.9999%)of pathogenic bacteria Virus reduction Up to log 5 (99.999%) Protozoa reduction > log 4 (99.99%) Heavy metals and/or arsenic reduction >87.5 % removal of Arsenic, chromium, cadmium, molybdenum, vanadium, antimony, selenium, chromium VI and mercury. Maximum recommended influent turbidity level (NTU) This filter has been tested under 4.5 NTU Effluent turbidity levels (NTU) <.10 (> 97.8%) Maintenance schedule Black Berkey™ purification elements are re-cleanable. If the time it takes for the water to filter into the lower chamber substantially decreases, they should be removed, and held under cleaning running water while scrubbing lightly with a ScotchBrite® pad or toothbrush. More on how to maintain the system properly here. Manufacturer-specified lifetime volume (L) Black Berkey® Purification Elements have a life expectancy of up to 3,000 gallons of water per element. Safe water storage integration 0.95 L to 22.7 L, depending on filter size Design Specifications Berkey ® Systems come in 7 different sizes: Go kit, Light, Travel, Big, Royal, Imperial and Crown Berkey ® System. Each system consists of an upper and lower stainless steel chamber (except for the Light Berkey ® System which is make of non-BPA/BPS co-polyester). The filtering process is performed by Black Berkey® Purification Elements which come standard in pairs in all systems (except for the Go Berkey® Kit which comes with one element) and can be added in pairs up to 8 elements to speed up purification. Sizes of Berkey Filters with number of Black Berkey® Purification Elements. To use, water is poured from any source into the top chamber of the system, and purified water is dispensed from the lower chamber through a spigot. Depending on filter size, normal everyday use ranges from 1 to 12 people and up to 625 people during emergency situations. Storage capacity can range from 0.95 L to 22.7 L and average flow rate is between 3.8 L/hours and 98.4 L /hours. Dimensions: Go Berkey ® kit: 14¨x 4¨ (355.6 x 101.6mm); 2.4 Lb (weight empty); 0.95 L of storage capacity; 3.8 L/hour of average flow rate . Light Berkey ® System: 26.5¨x 9¨; 8 Lb (weight empty); 10.4 L of storage capacity; 15.2 – 26.5 L/hour of average flow rate. Travel Berkey ® System: 18¨x 7.5¨; 7 Lb (weight empty); 5.7 L of storage capacity; 10.4 L/hour of average flow rate. Big Berkey ® System: 19.25¨x 9¨; 7 Lb (weight empty); 8.5 L of storage capacity; 13.3 – 26.5 L/hours of average flow rate. Royal Berkey ® System: 23 x 9.5¨; 8 Lb (weight empty); 12.3 L of storage capacity; 15.1 – 26.5 L/hour of average flow rate. Imperial Berkey ® System: 26¨x 10¨; 10 Lb (weight empty); 17 L of storage capacity; 20.8 – 62.5 L/hours of average flow rate. Crown Berkey ® System: 30¨x 11¨; 12 Lb (weight empty); 22.7 L of storage capacity; 24.6 – 98.4 L/hours of average flow rate. Product Schematics Technical Support Costumers are asked to visit Berkey® by NMCL Customer Support & Service for defective products requiring repair or replacement under the terms of New Millennium Concepts product0s Limited Warranty and return policy. Replacement Components Replacements components include the Black Berkey® Purification Elements and other items, as well as accessories or enhancements such as the Berkey PF-2™ Fluoride and Arsenic Reduction Elements that may be installed on each Black Berkey® Purification Element. Lifecycle Each pair of Black Berkey® Purification Elements has a typical expected use of about 5-8 years (up to 6,000 gallons of treated water) before needing replacement and includes a 2 years limited prorated warranty. Other components such as the Berkey PF-2™ Fluoride and Arsenic Reduction Filters and all Berkey® accessories include a 6 month warranty. Manufacturer Specified Performance Parameters Berkey® systems equipped with Black Berkey®Purification Elements are designed to accomodate size of the user’s space, number of people served and water use. They are also made to remove up to 99.999% of viruses and 99.9999% of pathogenic bacteria, and to remove or dramatically reduce protozoa, trihalomethanes, inorganic minerals, heavy metals, pharmaceuticals, pesticides, VOCs, petroleum products, perfluorinated chemicals, rust, silt, sediment and even radiologicals. Designer specific performane targets include: powerful (filter), effortless, convenient,portable, durable, economical and proven. Vetted Performance Status Black Berkey Purification elements have been tested for biological and microbiological removal efficiency by Biological Consulting Services of North Florida Inc. and ANALYTICAL SERVICES, INC. and for chemical and physical contaminants reduction and/or removal by ENVIROTEK LABORATORIES, INC., QUALITY FILTER TESTING LABORATORY, LLC. and by Water Filter Labs. For a list of contaminant filtration specifications click here. For the link to laboratory tests results performed see here. Safety Potential hazards would be those related to the use of this product when damaged and is in need of replacement. When the carbon of the black filtering elements becomes exhausted, although the ceramic shell will continue to remove pathogenic bacteria and turbidity, the filter will be unable to reduce chemicals, foul taste and odors of the source water. Complementary Technical Systems The Berkey Purification systems do no need extra component to assist function. However there are accessories that can be bought to improve function and purification such as the Berkey PF-2™ Fluoride and Arsenic Reduction Elements. name: Bici Bomba Rosario de Agua
description: The Bici Bomba Rosario de Agua is a pedal-powered rope pump for retrieving water from wells.

taxonomy.sub_category: Bici Bomba Rosario de Agua Updated on December 22, 2023 · Created on August 27, 2017 Bici Bomba Rosario de Agua Open-source The Bici Bomba Rosario de Agua is a pedal-powered rope pump for retrieving water from wells. Developed By Bici-Tec Tested By Bici-Tec Content Partners Unknown Info Tags Open-source Snapshot Manufacturing & Delivery Performance & Use Research & Standards Feedback Product Description Bici Bomba Rosario de Agua is a pedal-powered rope pump used to collect water. The machine uses bicycle parts combined with a rope pump to obtain water from beneath, usually hand dug wells. Note: The manufacturer, Bici-Tec does not have a website and is only available on Facebook. Target SDGs SDG 6: Clean Water and Sanitation SDG 1: No Poverty Market Suggested Retail Price $277.00 Target Users (Target Impact Group) Household, Community Distributors / Implementing Organizations Bici-Tec distributes the product directly and works with community leaders to distribute it. Interview with representative Competitive Landscape Direct competitors include BluePump , Vergnet-Hydro 60-2000 Pump , and iDE Rope Pump . Regions Latin America Countries Guatemala, Mexico Manufacturing/Building Method Make to order, the building methods are half artisanal and half industrialized. Intellectural Property Type Open-source User Provision Model Direct from manufacturer, to place an order call Carlos Marroquin at +502 4080 1572 or reach out to the company through their Facebook page. Distributions to Date Status Over 500. Interview with representative Pump type Human-powered pump Power requirement (W) None Average flow rate (L/min at specific head) ~19 L/min at 30 m Maximum head (m) 30 m Diameter (cm) 2.54 cm Design Specifications The product is stationary and pedal powered, it requires no electricity to be operated. It is made from used bicycle parts such as pedals, frame, chains, and handles. The pedaling system is connected to a cement flywheel to facilitate continuous pedaling and uses sprockets’ diameter relation to vary speed and torque. The system is connected to rope which is lowered down into a well and drawn up through a long pipe with the bottom immersed in water. The system also consists of round plastic balls whose diameter matches the diameter of the pipe and which function as a seal that helps pull the water to the surface. Product Schematics Technical Support Bici-Tec trains users at the moment of delivering the product on how to give preventive maintenance. If needed Bici-Tec can also do repairs or provide additional technical support. Interview with representative Replacement Components Available at Bici-Tec and locally available at bike shops. Interview with representative Lifecycle The product can be recycled afterwards, to make more bici maquinas or at a common recycling center since most of the structure is steel. Manufacturer Specified Performance Parameters This product pumps between 5 to 10 gallons of water per minute from a 30 m well. Vetted Performance Status This product pumps about 220 L in 15 minutes (~4 gallons per minute) from a 14 m well. Safety Risk of winding due to exposed rotating components and medium rotational speed. Complementary Technical Systems Bici-Tec teaches, empowers, and encourages people to use pedal powered machines (bici maquinas) through their school Escuela De Bici-Tecnología Apropriada. Academic Research and References “Bici-Tec – Instalación de Bici-bomba de Agua y entrega de…,” Facebook, https://www.facebook.com/bicitecGT/posts/725333997573325/ “Bici-Tec,” Facebook, https://www.facebook.com/bicitecGT/ “Products,” Engineering For Change, https://www.engineeringforchange.org/solutions/products “Goal 6 | Department of Economic and Social Affairs,” United Nations, https://sdgs.un.org/goals/goal6 Compliance with regulations Unknown Evaluation methods Field tests Interview with representative) Other Information Video Leave a Reply You must be logged in to post a comment. name: Bioguaviare
description: Bioguaviare is a Colombian company that produces pulp and natural oils from wild fruits of the Amazon rainforest.

taxonomy.category: Bioguaviare Updated on August 23, 2024 · Created on April 3, 2022 Bioguaviare Upcoming Update Bioguaviare is a Colombian company that produces pulp and natural oils from wild fruits of the Amazon rainforest. Developed By Bioguaviare Tested By Bioguaviare Content Partners Unknown Info Tags Upcoming Update Snapshot Manufacturing & Delivery Performance & Use Research & Standards Feedback Product Description Bioguaviare is a Colombian company producing fruit pulp and natural oils from the wild fruits of the Amazon rainforest and supplying it internationally. It has developed a business model based on sustainable management of the forest as a strategy of social conservation and inclusion of producer communities as founding partners of the company. Target SDGs SDG 2: Zero Hunger SDG 1: No Poverty Target Users (Target Impact Group) Household Distributors / Implementing Organizations Bioguaviare and their suppliers. Regions Worldwide Countries Colombia Manufacturing/Building Method Bioguaviare obtains 100% their fruit from farmers and indigenous communities living in Guaviare. It is unknown as to where and how the fruit is processed. Intellectural Property Type Select Type User Provision Model Users can obtain the product by contacting the manufacturer. Distributions to Date Status Unknown. Design Specifications The fruit pulp specifications include: Sensory characteristics Bromatological characteristics (%) Physiochemical characteristics Frozen Asai Pulp : Fresh fruit, water, acidulant (citric acid), preservatives (sodium benzoate and potassium sorbate) With flexible packaging options: Transparent coextruded polyethylene bag with a capacity of 125 g. Transparent poly bag 3 gauge with a capacity of 1 - 5 kg Frozen Araza Pulp : Fresh fruit, preservatives (sodium benzoate and potassium sorbate) Frozen Buriti Pulp : Fresh fruit, water, preservatives (sodium benzoate and potassium sorbate) Product Schematics Technical Support Bioguaviare provides technical assistance for fruit collectors. Replacement Components The fruit pulp and oil products need to be supplied at regular intervals as per the demand. Lifecycle Frozen Asai Pulp : For 12 months at freezing temperature, without breaking the cold chain. Frozen Araza Pulp : 6 - 9 months at freezing temperature (+/- 18°C) and 3 months at refrigeration temperature (+/- 12°C) without breaking the cold chain. Frozen Buriti Pulp : 12 months at freezing temperature, without breaking the cold chain. Fresh fruit, water, preservatives (sodium benzoate and potassium sorbate). Manufacturer Specified Performance Parameters Performance targets include maintaining a high quality product, providing a new source of income for farmers in the region, and purchasing the fruit at fair prices. Vetted Performance Status Unknown. Safety Safety instructions include: Consume the product before the expiration date. Do not thaw and refreeze. Complementary Technical Systems The company's business model is based on sustainable management of the amazonian forests by means of conservation and social inclusion of local communities. Academic Research and References Technology innovation to açaí productive chain development at the Medio Juruá region, Amazonas, Brazil: main challenges and lessons learned Compliance with regulations Bioguaviare meets the following regulations: Decree 3075 of 1997 Resolution 2674 of 2013 Resolution 3929 of 2013 Resolution 2505 of 2004 Resolution 4506 of 2013 International Code of Practice - General Principles of Food Hygiene (CAC/ RCP 1-1969, Rev. 4- 2003). Other Information Bioguaviare has structured its business to be socially and environmentally responsible . The business model is helping peasants and indigenous people in the region. 100% of Bioguaviare fruits are obtains from farmers and indigenous communities Technical assistance provided by Bioguaviare Bioguaviare Performance targets Data sheet for Frozen Asai Pulp Data sheet for Frozen Araza Pulp Data sheet for Frozen Buriti Pulp Leave a Reply You must be logged in to post a comment. name: BlueBox RO DS
description: The BlueBox RO DS is a portable desalination water filtration system.

taxonomy.sub_category: BlueBox RO DS Updated on January 25, 2024 · Created on September 10, 2020 BlueBox RO DS The BlueBox RO DS is a portable desalination water filtration system. Developed By MuchMoreWater Tested By BlueBox RO DS Content Partners Unknown Info Tags Snapshot Manufacturing & Delivery Performance & Use Research & Standards Feedback Product Description The BlueBox RO DS is a compact desalination water filtration system that comes in several models, including 70 RO and 140 RO . Packaged in a portable travel case, the system utilizes a central reverse osmosis filter and a high-pressure pump to remove salt and microbial life from seawater and brackish water. Target SDGs SDG 6: Clean Water and Sanitation SDG 7: Affordable and Clean Energy Market Suggested Retail Price $18,072.00 Market Suggested Retail Price (Secondary Currency) 15,250 EUR Target Users (Target Impact Group) Household Distributors / Implementing Organizations UNICEF, WHO, DANIDA, Red Cross, FMV, DEMA, DSB Competitive Landscape Direct competitors include Spectra Aquifer 360 . Regions Worldwide Manufacturing/Building Method This product is manufactured and produced in Denmark. Intellectural Property Type Trademark User Provision Model This product is distributed by humanitarian organizations to individual communities in developing countries and disaster zones. Alternatively, the product can be purchased directly from the manufacturer. Distributions to Date Status Unknown Type of system Reverse osmosis filtration Manufacturer-specified water treatment rate (L/hr) 70 RO: 70 L/hr 140 RO: 140 L/hr Bacteria reduction 99.999999% Virus reduction 99.999999% Protozoa reduction 99.999999% Heavy metals and/or arsenic reduction 98.9% reduction for salts and minerals, metals reduction is unknown Power required 1.3 kW electric motor, included Safe water storage capacity (L) None Design Specifications The BlueBox RO DS systems take influent raw seawater or brackish water with a salt concentration of 2500 ppm or greater and pass it through a reverse osmosis filter to remove salt and other minerals as well as microbial life. The system is 900 mm x 600 mm x 400 mm and weighs 57 kg. The operating pressure is 55 bar and the filter purifies at the 5-micron level. The model comes in the 70 and 140 model sizes, each number referring to the flow rate of the system in L/hr. Technical Support Provided by manufacturer. Replacement Components 6-month replacement list: 5-micron filter, pickling solution powder Lifecycle Unknown Manufacturer Specified Performance Parameters This filter was designed to remove 100% of microbes and 98.9% of salts with an assumed influent salt ratio of 2500 ppm or greater. name: British Berkefeld Household Filter Kit
description: The British Berkefeld Household Filter kit is a gravity fed two-bucket ceramic candle filter for household use.

taxonomy.sub_category: British Berkefeld Household Filter Kit Updated on January 11, 2024 · Created on September 27, 2018 British Berkefeld Household Filter Kit The British Berkefeld Household Filter kit is a gravity fed two-bucket ceramic candle filter for household use. Developed By Doulton Tested By Centre for Affordable Water and Sanitation Technologies (CAWST) Humanitarian Innovation Fund (HIF) Content Partners Centre for Affordable Water and Sanitation Technologies (CAWST) Info Tags Snapshot Manufacturing & Delivery Performance & Use Research & Standards Feedback Product Description The British Berkefeld Household Filter Kit is a tabletop filter produced in the UK by Doulton. The kit includes two, three, or four ceramic candles, two 16 L buckets with snap-on lids, a dispensing tap for the storage container, the required hardware (such as washers and nuts) for installation, and a tool to measure the diameter of the candles. According to the manufacturer, kits sold for emergency response typically come with two silver-impregnated Sterasyl ceramic candles. Target SDGs SDG 6: Clean Water and Sanitation SDG 3: Good Health and Well-Being Target Users (Target Impact Group) Household, Community Distributors / Implementing Organizations British Berkefeld distributes the Household Filter kit via organizations such as UN Habitat and Lifewater Competitive Landscape Direct competitors include Katadyn Drip , Kohler Clarity Water Filter , Rainfresh MP4U Gravity Filter , Tulip Table Top Filter , and Nazava Water Filter . Regions Worldwide Manufacturing/Building Method Made in the UK by Doulton with these specifications: 2 BPA free food grade polypropylene containers with snap on lids Available with 2, 3 or 4 ceramic filter candles 7” long x 2” diameter with long threaded mount 1 Black Rubber Sealing Washer for each candle fitted 1 Black Plastic Wing Nut for each candle fitted 1 Plastic dispensing tap with plastic threaded nut and 2 black rubber washers. Intellectural Property Type Select Type User Provision Model Users can obtain the product from distributing NGOs and Aid Agencies or directly from the manufacturer. Distributions to Date Status The Household filter kit has been exported in pallets of 100 to more than 140 countries for disaster response. Type of filter Ceramic candle filter Primary material of construction Silver-impregnated ceramic Additives No Manufacturer-specified flow rate (L/hr) 2 L Bacteria reduction 4 log Virus reduction Unknown Protozoa reduction Unknown Heavy metals and/or arsenic reduction Unknown Maximum recommended influent turbidity level (NTU) <0.5 NTU Effluent turbidity levels (NTU) Unknown Maintenance schedule Scrub filter candles when flow is reduced Manufacturer-specified lifetime volume (L) 10,000 liters Safe water storage integration 8 liters Design Specifications The kit includes two, three, or four ceramic candles, two 16 L buckets with snap-on lids, a dispensing tap for the storage container, the required hardware (such as washers and nuts) for installation, and a tool to measure the diameter of the candles. According to the manufacturer, kits sold for emergency response typically come with two silver-impregnated Sterasyl ceramic candles. Product Schematics Technical Support Contact the manufacturer Replacement Components The regular Sterasyl filter cartridge containing only silver-impregnated ceramic is not available for purchase online, but the upgraded Super Sterasyl, containing activated carbon for additional chemicals removal is available for sale online for $22. Lifecycle It is recommended to change the filter cartridges every 12 months for the daily use of a 4 person. Manufacturer Specified Performance Parameters Each Sterasyl filter candle is designed to filter 10,000 liters and last a family 12 months. The Household filter kit contains 2, 3 or 4 ceramic filter candles, with an expected flow rate of output of up to 1 liter per candle per hour. Vetted Performance Status The performance was tested as part of the Humanitarian Innovation Fund’s Emergency Household Water Filter Challenge. Safety No safety risks. name: BushProof Biosand Filter
description: BushProof biosand filters are a slow sand filtration product for household use.

taxonomy.sub_category: BushProof Biosand Filter Updated on January 10, 2024 · Created on August 27, 2015 BushProof Biosand Filter Open-source BushProof biosand filters are a slow sand filtration product for household use. Developed By Dr. David Manz, University of Calgary Tested By Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) Content Partners Unknown Info Tags Open-source Snapshot Manufacturing & Delivery Performance & Use Research & Standards Feedback Product Description BushProof provides trainings to make Biosand filters , which are a slow sand filtration product for household use. Biosand filtration is an appropriate and low-cost method for household water treatment and is proven to reduce the incidence of diarrhoea. Target SDGs SDG 6: Clean Water and Sanitation Market Suggested Retail Price $1,687.00 Target Users (Target Impact Group) Household Distributors / Implementing Organizations BushProof Competitive Landscape Direct competitors include PurAll Water Purification , DHAN Biosand Filters , Ideas at Work Biosand Filter , and Hydraid® BioSand Filter . Countries Afghanistan, Congo (Brazzaville), Kenya, Madagascar, Pakistan, Sri Lanka, Sudan, Uganda Manufacturing/Building Method Individual production by artisans and trained builders using locally available materials such as sand, water and cement. BushProof sells metal moulds for casting concrete housings in which the biosand is placed. There are also guidelines for producing moulds. Intellectural Property Type Open-source User Provision Model BushProof Biosand filters are sold through trained artisans and builders. BushProof trains individuals to make the systems and sells the moulds required to manufacture the filters. Distributions to Date Status As of March 2004, BushProof has sold 670 units in Madagascar, Africa: 600 for rural households and 70 in urban households. Target use case Household use Manufacturer-specified flow rate (L/hr) 25 Bacteria reduction 99% E. Coli Protozoa reduction Unknown Virus reduction Unknown Heavy metals and/or arsenic reduction Unknown Maximum recommended influent turbidity level (NTU) 0-10 ( Tested under 176.0 – 360.0) Effluent turbidity levels (NTU) Filters treating highly turbid source water (176.0 – 360.0 NTU) were observed to remove between 98.7 and 99.8% of turbidity. Manufacturer-specified volume between cleaning (L) ? 20 Material of construction Concrete (cement, sand, gravel) Design Specifications BushProof Biosand filters work according to the principles of a general BioSand Filter (BSF) whereby layers of sand and gravel are housed in a concrete container. The water level is maintained above the sand layer by setting the height of the outlet pipe. This shallow water layer allows a bioactive layer to grow on top of the sand, which contributes to the reduction of disease-causing organisms. A diffuser plate with holes in it is placed on the top of the sand layer to prevent disruption of the biolayer when water is added to the system. To use the BSF, users pour water into the BSF, and collect finished water out of the outlet pipe into a bucket. The BushProof Biosand Filters relies on intermittently operated BSF technology. In this design, the drain pipe is raised 1-8 cm above the sand level, which ensures that the water level is just above the sand. Even when water is not continually added to the filter, oxygen can still permeate into the water to reach the bioactive layer by diffusion across this shallow layer of standing water. When the flow rate becomes unacceptably low, the filter can be cleaned by removing accumulated dirt from the top few centimeters of sand. There are several ways to do this, but all disturb the biological layer which results in less effective filtration for some time. Some methods are less disruptive than others. Product Schematics Technical Support BushProof-trained artisans provide technical support, and an instruction booklet can be accessed online. Replacement Components All materials are locally available and can be sourced by the builder. Lifecycle About 4 years according to research conducted by Medair, Kenya Manufacturer Specified Performance Parameters Intermittently-operated slow sand filters can be made as small units that supply clean water for a family. Under ideal conditions, a biosand filter of this size produces 1 liter of clean water per minute. Even at a much slower flow rate, however, the filter can be practical. A filter that fills a 20-liter jerry can in two hours can be convenient to a family, even though this equals a flow rate of only 0.16 liters per minute. Vetted Performance Status Safe Household Drinking Water via Biosand Filtration Pilot Project Evaluation and Feasibility Study of a Biosand Pitcher Filter Water, advocacy, sanitation and hygiene: Lessons learnt from Tearfund’s global water, advocacy, sanitation and hygiene programme 2007–2012. Written by Murray Burt and Bilha Joy Keiru. Safety Concrete filters can be heavy and difficult to move and transport. Complementary Technical Systems Safe water storage containers. Academic Research and References Significant research has been conducted on BSF technology. A comprehensive list is available via CAWST. Additional examples include: Sangya-Sangam K. Tiwari, et al. Intermittent slow sand filtration for preventing diarrhoea among children in Kenyan households using unimproved water sources: randomized controlled trial . Tropical Medicine & International Health Volume 14, Issue 11, pages 1374–1382, November 2009 Michael Lea. Clearinghouse, Low-cost Water Treatment Technologies for Developing Countries, Ottawa, Ontario, Canada. Biological Sand Filters: Low-Cost Bioremediation Technique for Production of Clean Drinking Water Current Protocols in Microbiology 1G.1.1-1G.1.28, May 2008 “Track Record,” BushProof, 02-Oct-2013. Available: https://bushproof.com/about/track-record/ “Household Biosand Filtration,” BushProof, 04-Oct-2013. Available: https://bushproof.com/products/trainings/household-biosand-filtration/ “Goal 6,” Sdgs.un.org. Available: https://sdgs.un.org/goals/goal6 Adriaan, E. Fewster, B. Org, and A. Mol, Biosandfilter.org, 2007. Available: https://www.biosandfilter.org/wp-content/uploads/2017/05/BioSandFilter_Mould_Construction_Guidelines-1.pdf “Downloads,” Biosand filter, 09-May-2017. Available: https://biosandfilter.org/downloads/ Mit.edu. Available: https://web.mit.edu/watsan/Docs/Student%20Theses/Nepal/Pincus2003.pdf “Sand as a filter media,” Biosand filter, 22-May-2017. Available: https://biosandfilter.org/biosand-filter/filter-media/sand-as-a-filter-media/ “Operation & Maintenance,” Biosand filter, 22-May-2017. Available: https://biosandfilter.org/biosand-filter/operation-maintenance/ Biosand filter. Available: https://biosandfilter.org/biosandfilter/files/webfiles/Field_Sand_Sieve_Analysis_Instructions.pdf Mit.edu. Available: https://web.mit.edu/watsan/Docs/Student%20Theses/Ghana/2009/Thesis%20-%20Final,%20Clair%20Collin,%205-15-09.pdf Compliance with regulations Although Biosand filters have been tested to reduce pathogen load in drinking water, they do not consistently comply with WHO standards for drinking water. Evaluation methods Field evaluations. Examples include, Medair Kenya – Biosand filter project evaluation report Results showed that filters were still performing very well, producing drinking water of acceptable purity to the majority of households that bought one 4 years prior. name: Caminos de Agua Ceramic filters
description: Ceramic filters by Caminos de Agua are low-cost household filters available in Mexico.

taxonomy.sub_category: Caminos de Agua Ceramic filters Updated on January 11, 2024 · Created on July 21, 2017 Caminos de Agua Ceramic filters Open-source Ceramic filters by Caminos de Agua are low-cost household filters available in Mexico. Developed By Caminos de Agua Tested By CATIS-Mexico Columbia University CONAGUA, Mexico EcoLaboratorios, S.A State Water Commission of Queretaro Mexico University College of London Content Partners Unknown Info Tags Open-source Snapshot Manufacturing & Delivery Performance & Use Research & Standards Feedback Product Description Caminos de Agua Ceramic filters purify water through a clay filter with the pore size between 0.6 – 3.0 microns, producing 1 L/hr of filtered water. Target SDGs SDG 6: Clean Water and Sanitation SDG 3: Good Health and Well-Being Market Suggested Retail Price $29.39 Target Users (Target Impact Group) Household, Community Distributors / Implementing Organizations Camino de Agua Competitive Landscape Direct competitors include CeraMaji , Tulip Table Top Filter , CT Filtron , and Tunsai (Rabbit) Ceramic Water Purifier . Countries Mexico Manufacturing/Building Method The product is manufactured by blending a local clay mix from Guanajuato state, Mexico, with carefully screened sawdust from a local sawmill. After water is added to the clay, the mixture it is pressed into molds. Once the molded filters have dried, they are packed into a kiln and fired, burning out the sawdust to achieve the desired porosity. The finished ceramic filters are impregnated with liquid colloidal silver. The filters are tested for quality control visually and with a variety of flow tests. Finally, the filters are flushed with water to remove any residual silver or loosely-bound minerals. All manufacture, processing, and testing happens in Mexico. Correspondence with the manufacturer Intellectural Property Type Open-source User Provision Model Caminos de Agua works with local communities and partnerships to provide open source products. Distributions to Date Status As of July 2018, 2336 units have been distributed. Type of filter Ceramic candle filter Primary material of construction Fired clay Additives None Manufacturer-specified flow rate (L/hr) 1 L Bacteria reduction > 4 Log (> 99.99%) for E. coli ., coliforms, and total coliforms Virus reduction Unknown Protozoa reduction Unknown Heavy metals and/or arsenic reduction Unknown Maximum recommended influent turbidity level (NTU) This filter has been tested under conditions of 483 NTU and 615 NTU. Effluent turbidity levels (NTU) 0.71 NTU (with influent tubidity levels of 483 NTU) and 1 NTU (with influent tubidity levels of 615 NTU). Maintenance schedule Scrub the ceramic filter lightly with a toothbrush when the flowrate of the filter reduces or once every three months minimum. Manufacturer-specified lifetime volume (L) 26280 L Safe water storage integration Not available Design Specifications The filter is made from fired clay and attached to a 20 L water jug that gravity-feeds water through the ceramic filter. The column shape is more compact and robust than traditional ceramic pot filters, allowing for easier shipping and adaptability. The walls of the filter are 40% wider than most ceramic pot filters (0.5 in vs. 0.725 in). The filters are formed in a simple mold, with readily available materials, instead of a large mechanized press like traditional pot filters. Technical Support Provided by Caminos de Agua. Replacement Components The ceramic filter itself can be replaced through Caminos de Agua. Lifecycle Approximately 26,280 liters (25 L/day for 3-5 years) Manufacturer Specified Performance Parameters Testing results published by the manufacturer indicate surpassing all Mexican and US-EPA drinking water regulations. Vetted Performance Status Laboratory test results and reports on community pilot projects are available. Safety Safety precautions while installation and using the filter are available in the manual. Complementary Technical Systems Safe water storage unit. Academic Research and References I. Adler, H. Zhang, M. Canales, Evaluation of a silver-impregnated ceramic filter for small-scale rainwater purification using viral and bacterial indicators . University of College London, UK. Report by Columbia University , May 2013. U. S. Epa and OW, “National Primary Drinking Water Regulations,” 2015. “Caminos de Agua,” Caminos de Agua. taxonomy.sub_category: Caminos de la Villa Updated on June 26, 2024 · Created on September 16, 2020 Caminos de la Villa Caminos de la Villa is an interactive data visualization platform that allows users to follow-up and monitor urbanization processes in slums. Developed By ACIJ Wingu Tested By ACIJ Wingu Content Partners Unknown Info Tags Snapshot Manufacturing & Delivery Performance & Use Research & Standards Feedback Product Description Caminos de la Villa is a civic technology platform that allows users to follow-up and monitor the urbanization process carried out by the government of Argentina in the slums of Buenos Aires. Users can access detailed information, and download documents or pictures about the public works in each neighborhood. The platform also allows the citizens of those areas to report claims and add points of interest in their neighborhood.
